channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: The FBI arrested a man who's been charged with planning an attack on the New York Stock Exchange
item.description: A Florida man was arrested Wednesday and charged with a plot to “reboot” the U.S. government by planting a bomb at the New York Stock Exchange this week and detonating it with a remote-controlled device, according to the FBI.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Chris Stapleton is the star again early at the Country Music Association Awards
item.description: Post Malone and Morgan Wallen’ are the flashiest nominees. But it was Chris Stapleton's night early on at the Country Music Association Awards on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Nearly $100 million awarded to the family of a man fatally shot in his apartment by an officer
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — The family of a man shot and killed by a Dallas police officer who said she mistook his apartment for her own was awarded nearly $100 million Wednesday in a federal civil trial.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump chooses former acting Attorney General Matt Whitaker as NATO ambassador
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump said Wednesday that he has chosen former acting Attorney General Matt Whitaker to serve as U.S. ambassador to NATO, the bedrock Western alliance that the president-elect has expressed skepticism about for years.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Man convicted of killing Laken Riley sentenced to life in prison without parole
item.description: ATHENS, Ga. (AP) — The Venezuelan man convicted of killing Georgia nursing student Laken Riley has been sentenced to life in prison without the possibility of parole in a case that became a flashpoint in the national debate over immigration.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: CMA Awards: 2024 Winners | Live Results
item.description: Keep up with all the biggest winners and moments from Country Music’s Biggest Night™ at the 58th Annual CMA Awards live from Nashville, TN!
item.dc:creator: Sebastian Posey, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florist gets death threats after refusing to serve Trump voters
item.description: The owner posted on social media that the business is now temporarily closed after receiving several death threats. She also made all of her social media accounts private.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Knox, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate rejects effort from Bernie Sanders to block some weapons for Israel over Gaza deaths
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate on Wednesday rejected an attempt by Sen. Bernie Sanders Wednesday to block sales of offensive weapons to Israel for its war in Gaza over mounting civilian deaths there.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Meta's Threads app launches custom feeds amid Bluesky surge
item.description: The rollout came just days after Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg announced last week that Threads was testing the new feature.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s hunt for Treasury secretary down to the wire
item.description: The jockeying for President-elect Trump’s pick to run the all-important Treasury Department is coming down to the wire. Trump’s economic team will be tasked with imposing an aggressive tariff regime meant to shake up global trade while keeping financial markets calm amid the disruption.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Human smuggling trial witness says he was separated from a family hours before they froze to death
item.description: FERGUS FALLS, Minn. (AP) — An Indian national who survived a treacherous trek across the U.S.-Canada border in blizzard conditions testified Wednesday that he got separated from a family of four shortly before they froze to death.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New study shows voting for Native Americans is harder than ever
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY, Okla. (AP) — A new study has found that systemic barriers to voting on tribal lands contribute to substantial disparities in Native American turnout, particularly for presidential elections.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Meta's Threads app launches custom feeds amid Bluesky surge
item.description: The rollout came just days after Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg announced last week that Threads was testing the new feature.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge dismisses wrongful death lawsuit that Gabby Petito's parents filed against Moab, Utah police
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — A Utah judge on Wednesday dismissed a wrongful death lawsuit that Gabby Petito's parents filed against the city of Moab alleging police did not do enough during a traffic stop to protect their daughter from the man who killed her weeks later.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pamela Hayden, longtime 'Simpsons' voice actor, hangs up her mic
item.description: “It’s been an honor and a joy to have worked on such a funny, witty, and groundbreaking show,” Hayden said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Sing-along screenings of 'Wicked' coming soon as AMC issues strict warning to fans
item.description: Fans eager to belt out hits like "Defying Gravity" and "Popular" in theaters during the highly anticipated "Wicked" movie musical will need to hold off — for now.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Yakubovsky, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Georgia election workers seek more penalties against Giuliani, saying he continues to defame them
item.description: Two former Georgia election workers who won a $148 million defamation judgment against Rudy Giuliani asked a judge Wednesday to penalize him even further for continuing to falsely accuse them of tampering with ballots during the 2020 presidential election.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hospital 'walk-in' climbs into ceiling, gets stuck on HVAC
item.description: "We learned the subject was a walk-in who was believed to be under the influence of drugs," police said.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, NewsNation

item.title: Fugitive arrested after Chicago standoff blocks away from DNC is being returned to Mississippi
item.description: At the time of the escape, the fugitive was set to make a court appearance on Mississippi charges of attempted murder and armed robbery.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Here's how much Thanksgiving dinner will cost this year
item.description: Here's which Thanksgiving dishes will cost more, and less, this year, according to the American Farm Bureau Federation.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KRON4

item.title: Unraveling the autopsy expertise of Texas medical examiners
item.description: Only 14 of 254 counties in Texas employ a medical examiner, while the rest rely on a justice of the peace.
item.dc:creator: Eric Henrikson, NewsNation

item.title: Texas offers Trump land on US-Mexico border for potential mass deportations
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — Texas is offering a parcel of rural ranchland along the U.S.-Mexico border to use as a staging area for potential mass deportations under President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk, Ramaswamy lay out plans for 'mass' federal layoffs, rule rollbacks under Trump
item.description: Tech entrepreneurs Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy said Wednesday that their brand-new government efficiency panel will identify “thousands” of regulations for President-elect Donald Trump to eliminate, which they argue will justify “mass head-count reductions” across government.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KRON4

item.title: Most say they probably won't get updated COVID vaccine: Survey
item.description: It has been over four years since the U.S. began dealing with COVID-19, with the spread of the virus causing mass shutdowns within multiple industries and people around the world being forced to stay in their homes.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: 2nd Bee Gees drummer dies within a week
item.description: Colin Petersen was a child actor who starred in the classic 1956 film "Smiley," which ended up being his nickname.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, NewsNation

item.title: Playground inspired by 4-year-old who battled rare disorder
item.description: "The loss of Sebastian is indescribable. The pain that we still live with two and a half years later, but also seeing the playground and being able to honor him brings us joy,” said his mother.
item.dc:creator: Jack Shea, NewsNation

item.title: Susan Smith denied parole 30 years after drowning sons by rolling car into South Carolina lake
item.description: Susan Smith made national headlines in 1994 after she rolled her car into a lake with both of her children strapped in their seats.
item.dc:creator: Dustin George, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida man files lawsuit against Netflix over Tyson-Paul fight streaming issues
item.description: A class action lawsuit was filed against Netflix over alleged streaming issues during the fight between legendary brawler Mike Tyson and former YouTuber Jake Paul.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: Explicit traffic signs appear in Colorado, police investigating
item.description: The signs used explicit language telling drivers to "slow down [expletive]" and "put down the phone [expletive].
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, NewsNation

item.title: Operation Santa: How to adopt a child's letter to the North Pole
item.description: The U.S. Postal Service's Operation Santa program is underway, allowing generous postal service customers to adopt letters from less fortunate children and families, and send them gifts in time for the holidays.
item.dc:creator: Marisa Rodriguez, WGN 9

item.title: Prosecution rests in trial over death of Georgia nursing student Laken Riley
item.description: ATHENS, Ga. (AP) — Prosecutors finished calling witnesses Tuesday in the trial of the man accused of killing Georgia nursing student Laken Riley, and the defense began presenting its case but quickly ran into snags. Jose Ibarra, 26, is charged with murder and other crimes in Riley’s February death.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Viral social media posts claim another stimulus check is coming. Don’t believe it
item.description: No new economic stimulus payments are scheduled despite viral social media claiming otherwise, according to the IRS website. “The IRS has issued all first, second and third Economic Impact Payments. You can no longer use the Get My Payment application to check your payment status.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Former 'American Idol' contestant pleads guilty in deadly DUI crash
item.description: Former "American Idol" contestant Caleb Kennedy has pleaded guilty to driving under the influence during a deadly 2022 crash in South Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Robert Cox, KRON4

item.title: Hacker gains access to testimony in Gaetz investigations: reports
item.description: A hacker gained access to witness testimony related to investigations into former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.), who resigned from Congress last week after President-elect Trump tapped him for attorney general, multiple news outlets reported Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Regina Zilbermints, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Diddy tried to reach out to witnesses from jail, prosecutors allege
item.description: Combs has repeatedly "flouted rules" so he can "improperly impact the outcome of his case," prosecutors said.
item.dc:creator: Kelley Connors, Fox 5

item.title: Police seek witnesses, cell phone video after gunfire during a parade in New Orleans kills 2
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Deadly shootings at a signature New Orleans cultural event — a neighborhood “second-line” parade — left authorities pleading with witnesses to come forward with information or cell phone video while seeking to reassure the public that the weekend bloodshed was an aberration in a c
item.dc:creator: KEVIN McGILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump taps Brendan Carr to be FCC chairman
item.description: President-elect Trump is tapping Brendan Carr of the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to lead the agency.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5
